title: T-056 — Warehouse cross-module contract tests
prompt:
# T-056 — Warehouse cross-module contract tests

PRD: `docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md`.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Add contract tests for Warehouse dependencies with Settings, Technical, Planning, Scanner, Production, Quality, and Shipping.

## Implementation contract
Verify only documented contracts are consumed/produced: Settings rule/locations/printers, Technical item/shelf-life/catch-weight fields, Planning PO/TO/WO handoffs, Scanner endpoints, Production consume/output events, Quality status history, and Shipping Phase 2 shelf-life rule consumer contract.

## Files (scope)
- packages/contracts/warehouse/** [create]
- apps/web/e2e/contracts/warehouse-cross-module.spec.ts [create]
- _meta/atomic-tasks/05-warehouse/coverage.md

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given Planning emits PO/TO/WO fixtures, then Warehouse GRN, reservation, and consume contract tests accept only documented statuses and fields.
2. Given Scanner calls warehouse endpoints, then LP lookup, barcode validate, FEFO suggest, lock, and consume-to-WO contracts return documented response shapes and error codes.
3. Given Quality changes qa_status, then Warehouse gates pick/consume/ship according to V-WH-LP-006 without owning the transition definitions.
4. Given 11-SHIPPING reads shelf_life_rules, then Warehouse exposes the P1 CRUD/read contract while enforcement remains marked as downstream Phase 2.

## Test strategy
- RED: Pact/OpenAPI-style fixtures fail for undocumented fields/statuses.
- GREEN: integration tests run against seeded contract fixtures with tenant boundaries.
- Run pnpm test --filter contracts -- warehouse and pnpm playwright test --grep @warehouse-contracts.

## Risk red lines
- Do not introduce D365 as source of truth.
- Do not duplicate Quality transition ownership in Warehouse.
- Do not make Shipping enforcement part of Warehouse P1 execution.

cross_module_dependencies.contract: rule_registry, warehouses/locations/printers, feature flags, tenant security policy
PO/TO/WO read models, RM root reservations, WO release/cancel hooks
LP lookup, barcode validation, lock protocol, FEFO suggestion, consume-to-WO
WO material consumption and output LP creation events
